title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths · Incident Defense
summary: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a least-privilege management access plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Net Site Client Vpn · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A site-to-site VPN automatically authorizes every device on both networks. This fails the Net Site Client Vpn contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Inband Outofband applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass.
